expectations.text: Explicitly recognizes the flat-profile situation as the mature case
expectations.evidence: Title 'You Have a Flat Profile — That's the Mature Case'. Lines 5-7: 'This is the flat profile, and it's the mature case: it means you've already eliminated the obvious hotspots, and what's left is the long tail of many small contributors with no single dominant win.'

expectations.text: Advises accepting it or making an architectural change rather than grinding many tiny items for a big win
expectations.evidence: 'Your two real options': 'Option 1: Accept the profile' (line 24) and 'Option 2: Make an architectural change' (line 53). Lines 84-85: 'What you should not do is spend weeks grinding many tiny items hoping they sum to a big win — that's the worst-ROI path on a flat profile.'

expectations.text: Mentions measuring work-per-unit-time rather than raw throughput
expectations.evidence: Section header line 89: 'Crucial: measure work per unit time, not raw throughput.' Lines 92-94: 'Don't evaluate performance as raw throughput (fps, draw-calls/sec) in isolation — measure work and features delivered per unit time.' Reinforced by the 120fps-rich-scene vs 2000fps-empty-room comparison (lines 96-102).

expectations.text: Explicitly recognizes the flat-profile situation as the mature case
expectations.evidence: Line 3 header 'First, recognize what this is: the mature case'. Line 5: 'A flat profile is not a failure to find the bottleneck — it's what a well-optimized program looks like... Treat this as the mature stage of optimization, not a fresh problem to grind.'

expectations.text: Advises accepting it or making an architectural change rather than grinding many tiny items for a big win
expectations.evidence: Line 9 header 'Don't grind the tiny items hoping for a big win'. Line 13 'You have two legitimate responses: 1. Accept it (line 15) 2. Make an architectural change (line 21).' Line 11: 'Halving a 0.5% cost buys you 0.25%. Doing that fifty times is a massive, bug-prone effort... approximately zero.'

expectations.text: Mentions measuring work-per-unit-time rather than raw throughput
expectations.evidence: Line 30 header 'Measure work-per-unit-time, not raw throughput'. Line 32: 'track the rate of useful work per unit time instead'. Line 36: 'Useful work per millisecond... An architectural change is validated when these improve... not when one function's raw number drops.'

notes: All 3 assertions pass in BOTH configs - non-discriminating. Baseline reproduced 'mature case', accept-or-architectural-change, and work-per-unit-time even though its snapshot lacked the exact terminology.
No discriminating assertion; the eval omits the JB-attribution check present in game-patterns/game-rendering. Grader recommends adding one.
